info.title: Swings API
info.description: Swings public + admin HTTP API. Mutating endpoints are annotated; GETs are still fully callable but not listed here (see FDN-02 scope).

200.description: Subscription scheduled to cancel at period end

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Base template id (read for key/channel/locale lookup)

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by lifecycle state. Omit to return every row.

post.summary: Public ingest for SPA analytics (optional Bearer links session to logged-in user).

200.description: Reset email dispatched if account exists

post.summary:
POST /api/csp-report — accept a browser violation report, log it, and
return 204 No Content. Never exposes internal state or reflects the body.

200.description: Subscription scheduled to cancel at period end

NotificationPreference.description:
A (user, category, channel) preference row as stored in
`notification_preferences`.

OutboxRowDto.description:
Paginated outbox row. Kept in sync with [`OutboxRecord`] but serialises the
status as a lowercase string for frontend friendliness.

OutboxStatus.description:
Lifecycle states for an outbox row. Mirrors the `CHECK` constraint in
`019_outbox.sql`.

PaginatedOutboxResponse.description:
OpenAPI wrapper so the snapshot carries a concrete `PaginatedResponse<OutboxRowDto>`
schema without bleeding the generic into `ApiDoc`.

PreferenceUpdate.description: Input shape for the bulk set endpoint. Missing timezone defaults to UTC.

RenderedTemplate.description:
Output of [`Template::render`]. Kept narrow so channels do not have to
re-parse Tera output.

Template.description:
A concrete template row loaded from `notification_templates`.

`body_compiled` is maintained by the admin API at save time (currently a
straight copy of `body_source` until MJML compilation lands in FDN-09);
rendering reads `body_compiled` so the hot path never re-parses MJML.
